If the sentiment of the delegates
to the Central Labor union can
be accepted as evidence, the local
unions still feel that Industrial
peace in this city is not yet Sc
cured. The Washington Water
Power company, as usual, Is the
cause of friction. That labor-fight-
ing concern was up for discussion
again last night, and the Central
union delegates expressed them
selves in favor of attempting by ev-
cry legitimate means to persuade
the public from patronizing the
cars Of the local monopoly. This
brought up the question If any
other car service could be secured
to the fair grounds. Delegates
from the Electrical Workers union
stated that the Traction company
would run cars closer to the fair
grounds than the Washington
Water Power company cars, and is
al oil W SNC pSt-
ter service.


Organizer j. w. Schrader, of the
Carpenters union, made an inter-
esting address. e is organizer for
Washington, Idaho and Oregon.
Among other things he was sur
prised at the intelligent and ex-
peditious manner in which the bust
ness coming before the central
body was handled The speaker
predicted that if the central body
would always work with the same
dispatch it would have time to take
up other subjects than those per-
tanning to local labor with the OBJ
ject of educating workingmen on
the great economic questions now
confronting the nation.
